docs.title: docpilot Wiki
docs.text: Welcome to the docpilot wiki — an MCP server providing semantic documentation search across 80+ frameworks and technologies for AI coding agents.

docs.title: Pages
docs.text:
• Getting Started
  • Supported Frameworks
  • Adding a New Framework
  • Architecture
  • Configuration
  • API Reference
  • Contributing

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: docpilot exposes two MCP tools via stdio transport.

docs.title: searchDocs
docs.text:
Semantic search across all indexed documentation.

Parameter Type Required Description query string ✅ Natural-language search query framework string ❌ Filter by framework ID (e.g., "go-fiber", "spring-boot", "hibernate") frameworkVersion string ❌ Filter by version (e.g., "4.0", "3.36") projectDir string ❌ Path to project directory for auto-detection maxResults integer ❌ Max results (default: 4, max: 50)

docs.title: Framework Auto-Detection
docs.text:
When projectDir is provided, docpilot scans for project files to boost relevant results:

File Detected Framework go.mod Go (Fiber/Gin/Echo based on imports) pom.xml Spring Boot / Quarkus build.gradle Spring Boot / Quarkus package.json NestJS / Express / React / Angular Cargo.toml Rocket / Actix / Axum requirements.txt FastAPI / Django / Flask *.csproj ASP.NET Core

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
{
  "name": "searchDocs",
  "arguments": {
    "query": "hibernate second level cache configuration",
    "framework": "hibernate",
    "maxResults": 5
  }
}

docs.title: listFrameworks
docs.text:
Returns all indexed frameworks with their IDs, languages, and versions.

Parameter Type Required Description (none) — — No parameters

docs.title: Example Response
docs.text:
[
  {"id": "hibernate", "language": "Java", "version": "7.0"},
  {"id": "spring-boot", "language": "Java", "version": "4.0"},
  {"id": "go-fiber", "language": "Go", "version": "3.3"}
]

docs.title: Step 1: Create a Scraper
docs.text:
Create a new file in ingestion/src/scrapers/:

from .github_base import GitHubDocsScraper


class MyFrameworkScraper(GitHubDocsScraper):
    repo = "org/repo"              # GitHub repo
    branch = "main"                # Branch to fetch from
    source = "my-framework"        # Unique ID
    version = "1.0"                # Framework version
    language = "python"            # Programming language
    topics_prefix = "myframework"  # Comma-separated topic tags
    categories = "web,python"      # Comma-separated categories
    paths = [                      # Doc files to scrape
        "docs/guide.md",
        "docs/api.md",
        "docs/configuration.md",
    ]

docs.title: Tips for Choosing Paths
docs.text:
• Look for markdown (.md) or AsciiDoc (.adoc) files in the repo's docs/ directory
  • Prioritize guides, tutorials, and API references over changelogs
  • Each path should have at least 100 characters of content
  • Start with 5-15 core documentation files

docs.title: Step 2: Register in CLI
docs.text:
Edit ingestion/src/cli.py:

# Add import
from .scrapers.my_framework import MyFrameworkScraper

# Add to SCRAPERS dict
SCRAPERS["my-framework"] = MyFrameworkScraper()

docs.title: Step 3: Run Ingestion
docs.text:
cd ingestion
python -m src.cli scrape --framework my-framework

This will: 1. Fetch docs from GitHub 2. Split into chunks by headers 3. Generate embeddings (BGE Small EN v1.5) 4. Write SQL to ingestion/output/my-framework-1.0.sql

docs.title: Step 4: Rebuild Docker Image
docs.text:
docker build -t framework-docs-mcp:latest .

The SQL file is automatically copied into the PostgreSQL init directory during the Docker build.

docs.title: Step 5: Update README
docs.text: Add your framework to the appropriate table in README.md.

docs.title: Query Flow
docs.text:
1. AI agent sends a natural-language query via MCP
  2. Quarkus embeds the query using BGE Small EN v1.5
  3. pgvector performs cosine similarity search (top 50 candidates)
  4. Results are reranked with metadata boosting (title, topics, framework match)
  5. Top N results returned to the agent

docs.title: Ingestion Pipeline (Build Time)
docs.text:
GitHub Repos → Python Scraper → Header-aware Chunking → BGE Embedding → SQL INSERT files
  1. Scrape: Fetch markdown/asciidoc from GitHub repos
  2. Chunk: Split by headers with recursive fallback (max ~1500 chars)
  3. Embed: Generate 384-dim vectors with BGE Small EN v1.5
  4. Write: Output as SQL INSERT statements

docs.title: Project Structure
docs.text:
docpilot/
├── pom.xml                          # Quarkus + langchain4j + MCP
├── Dockerfile
├── docker/
│   ├── entrypoint.sh                # Starts PG then Java server
│   ├── init.sql                     # Schema (pgvector)
│   └── zz-index.sql                 # ivfflat index
├── src/main/java/io/frameworkdocs/mcp/
│   ├── DocSearchTools.java          # MCP tool definitions
│   ├── FrameworkDetector.java       # Auto-detect from project files
│   ├── EmbeddingModelLoader.java    # BGE model loader
│   ├── ContainerManager.java        # PgVector connection
│   ├── SqlLoader.java               # Schema readiness
│   └── StartupObserver.java         # Warm-up
└── ingestion/
    ├── src/
    │   ├── cli.py                   # CLI commands
    │   ├── scraper.py               # BaseScraper + DocPage
    │   ├── chunker.py               # Header-aware splitting
    │   ├── embedder.py              # BGE embeddings
    │   ├── sql_writer.py            # SQL generation
    │   └── scrapers/                # One per framework
    └── output/                      # Generated .sql files

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Key Default Description docs-mcp.min-score 0.70 Minimum cosine similarity threshold docs-mcp.max-candidates 50 Number of candidates before reranking docs-mcp.default-max-results 4 Default results returned to agent

These are set in src/main/resources/application.properties.

docs.title: Lowering min-score
docs.text: Set below 0.70 to return more results (useful for broad queries). Risk: lower relevance.

docs.title: Raising max-candidates
docs.text: Increase to consider more documents before reranking. Marginally slower but may surface better matches for ambiguous queries.

docs.title: default-max-results
docs.text: Controls how many results are returned when the agent doesn't specify maxResults. The agent can override this up to 50.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Java 25
  • Python 3.12+
  • Docker

docs.title: Build from Source
docs.text:
# 1. Ingestion pipeline
cd ingestion
python3 -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
pip install -e .

# 2. Scrape a single framework
python -m src.cli scrape --framework hibernate

# 3. Scrape all frameworks
python -m src.cli scrape-all

# 4. Build MCP server
cd ..
./mvnw package -DskipTests -Dquarkus.package.jar.type=uber-jar

# 5. Build Docker image
docker build -t framework-docs-mcp:latest .

docs.title: Testing
docs.text:
printf '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"test","version":"1.0"}}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/initialized"}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":2,"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"searchDocs","arguments":{"query":"hibernate entity mapping"}}}
' \
  | docker run -i --rm framework-docs-mcp:latest

docs.title: How to Contribute
docs.text:
1. Add a framework — See Adding a New Framework
  2. Improve existing scrapers — Add more doc paths to cover missing topics
  3. Fix bugs — Submit a PR with a description of the issue
  4. Improve search quality — Tune chunking, embedding, or reranking logic

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text: • Docker installed

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: Add docpilot to your AI agent's MCP configuration:

docs.title: Claude Code
docs.text: claude mcp add framework-docs -- docker run -i --rm ghcr.io/shkna1368/docpilot:latest

docs.title: Kiro (~/.kiro/settings/mcp.json)
docs.text:
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "framework-docs": {
      "command": "docker",
      "args": ["run", "-i", "--rm", "ghcr.io/shkna1368/docpilot:latest"]
    }
  }
}

docs.title: VS Code / Copilot (.vscode/mcp.json)
docs.text:
{
  "servers": {
    "framework-docs": {
      "type": "stdio",
      "command": "docker",
      "args": ["run", "-i", "--rm", "ghcr.io/shkna1368/docpilot:latest"]
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Cursor / Windsurf / JetBrains
docs.text:
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "framework-docs": {
      "command": "docker",
      "args": ["run", "-i", "--rm", "ghcr.io/shkna1368/docpilot:latest"]
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Verify
docs.text:
Once configured, ask your agent something like:

"How do I set up routing in Go Fiber?"

The agent will call searchDocs and return accurate, up-to-date documentation.
